md:
Every few months/years, certain conditions happen to be simultaneously satisfied, and my mind (re)enters this specific frame X, which is immediately appreciably different from the one it usually occupies (Y), and despite having last been visited so long ago, bares all of the thoughts and memories synthesized within it upon previous visits with the ease and intimacy I expect of thoughts and memories synthesized at most a few days ago within Y.

I'll just write a few observations down (no particular order):

- Both X and Y seem dream-like from the perspective of the other (to a greater degree when thinking about my thoughts in X, when in Y).
- Transitioning from X to Y is abrupt and immediately impressed upon me, whereas the inverse is very gradual.
- The previously mentioned "satisfied conditions" include:
    - A particularly severe and prolonged depressive episode.
    - Losing myself in some immersive fictional work (happened with the novel "Blindsight", the game "SOMA", most recently the game "Clair Obscur", etc.) which are extremely relevant to the transition but are quickly cease to be the subject of my thoughts once I'm actually in Y.
    - A lack of requirements that force me to perform tasks within fixed timelines (exams or the like).
    - Sleep deprivation. 
    - there are probably a few more
- During the transition, I happen to sleep for inhumanly long periods of time (>12 hours) and experience vivid, life-like dreams strongly inspired by the specific work of fiction mentioned earlier (random side-note: since I don't wear my glasses when outside and have become used to the world being slightly blurry, movies and (certain) video games seem (almost overwhelmingly) "realer" than real life). 
- There is a general atmosphere of content, freeing melancholy within my mind and I spend a lot of time going through thoughts and memories from Y. Further, certain memories (not necessarily from Y, and most involving a certain person) become unusually important and simulating them over and over again gives me a lot of passive pleasure. It's not that I couldn't look at these memories when in X and recognize them to have some objective importance, and as I type this, I realize that I'm not sure why that recognition in X doesn't lead to me treating them with the care that I do in Y. I think it has something to do with me (when in X) not really believing (/forcing myself to not believe) that the person who lived through those memories was the same person recognizing them to be important in that moment. I could give as objective a detailed description of these memories in either state, but I seem to lack the ability to explain/feel all the emotions I experienced when living through those moments, when looking back through the grey windows of X.
- I lose the instinct to analyse my thoughts and actions along urgent, functional axes and am instead content to assume the role of a passive observer gently nudging my stream of thoughts from time to time. 
- I lose the burning (nigh pathological) desire to document my life that otherwise consumes me when in X.

I could write quite a bit more about this but what's the point
